title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NamespaceLifecycle is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NamespaceLifecycle is set
input_row.description: Reject creating objects in a namespace that is undergoing termination.
input_row.rationale: Setting admission control policy to NamespaceLifecycle ensures that objects cannot be created in non-existent namespaces, and that namespaces undergoing termination are not used for creating the new objects. This is recommended to enforce the integrity of the namespace termination process and also for the availability of the newer objects. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --disable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that does not include NamespaceLifecycle.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --disable-admission- plugins parameter to ensure it does not include NamespaceLifecycle. Default Value: By default, NamespaceLifecycle is set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/admission- controllers/#namespacelifecycle

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires checking the `--disable-admission-plugins` argument for the kube-apiserver, which can be verified through the API server's configuration.
- The audit mentions running a command to check the kube-apiserver process, which indicates that this can be automated by checking the configuration file used by the kube-apiserver.
- The configuration of the kube-apiserver is accessible via the Kubernetes API, specifically through the pod specification in the `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` file.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugins_namespace_lifecycle_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to retrieve the kube-apiserver pod specification.
- The command to check the kube-apiserver configuration can be done using:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].args}'
  ```
- Validate that the output does not include `--disable-admission-plugins=NamespaceLifecycle`.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The `--disable-admission-plugins` argument does not include `NamespaceLifecycle`.
  - **Fail:** The `--disable-admission-plugins` argument includes `NamespaceLifecycle`.
